name: Pentucket  Bank
description_short: Pentucket Bank is a community-focused bank offering deposit accounts, mortgages, business banking, and investment services across New Hampshire and Massachusetts locations.
description_long:
Pentucket Bank operates as a traditional community bank with a stated mission centered on customer satisfaction, business growth, and community strength. The bank positions itself as a 'River Run Bank' and emphasizes long-term relationships and local presence through multiple physical branches. The institution is FDIC-insured, providing deposit protection backed by the U.S. Government.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business banking services. On the consumer side, they provide checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs) with competitive rates, debit cards, mortgage products, and investment services through separate New Hampshire and Massachusetts divisions. For businesses, Pentucket offers business checking and savings accounts, business loans, enhanced online banking platforms, and dedicated business lending support. Current promotional rates advertised include 3.75% APY on both 5-month and 12-month CD specials.

Pentucket Bank distinguishes itself through emphasis on community banking and personalized service. The bank operates physical branch locations in both New Hampshire and Massachusetts, supports a mobile banking app, and provides digital assistant support ('Penny'). They offer separate online banking systems for personal and business customers, suggesting segmented service delivery. The bank markets itself around relationship-based banking rather than digital-only or discount-rate positioning.

As a traditional regional bank, Pentucket serves customers seeking established banking infrastructure and community-focused service. The website content is functional but limited in providing detailed rate comparisons, fee schedules, or specific loan terms. Prospective customers would need to visit branches or contact the bank directly for comprehensive product details. The bank appears well-suited for customers prioritizing local presence and relationship banking over purely digital convenience or aggressive rate shopping. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with promotional rates
Mortgage origination and servicing (purchase and refinance)
Personal online banking with digital access
Investment services (New Hampshire and Massachusetts divisions)
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Multiple physical branch locations in New Hampshire and Massachusetts for in-person service
Competitive CD rates currently at 3.75% APY for both 5-month and 12-month terms
Dedicated business banking division with specialized business loan team
Mobile app available for account management and transactions
Separate enhanced online banking platforms for business customers
Digital assistant ('Penny') available for customer support and inquiries
cons: Website provides limited information on checking/savings account fees or minimum balance requirements
Specific mortgage rates and terms not disclosed on website; requires direct contact or branch visit
No transparent fee schedule or comparison tools available online
Limited digital-first features for a modern banking option; primarily traditional branch-based model
Investment services split across New Hampshire and Massachusetts divisions may complicate cross-state access
best_for: Small business owners seeking personalized lending relationships and local banking presence
Customers in New Hampshire or Massachusetts prioritizing physical branch access and community banking
Savers looking for competitive CD rates with FDIC security
Borrowers seeking mortgage products with local underwriting and relationship-based service
diagnosis: Pentucket Bank is best suited for small-to-medium business owners and retail customers in New Hampshire and Massachusetts who value established banking infrastructure, physical branch relationships, and community-focused service over digital-only convenience or aggressive rate competition. The main caveat is that detailed pricing, fee structures, and specific product terms are not readily available online, requiring direct contact with the bank for comprehensive comparison and application.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
